summary: Move ready-sidecar post-handshake history and verify-session exchange into one shared Source Port transport, then exercise the accepted verify journal/effect composition only through the explicit native-test fake sidecar.
rationale: Issue #402 removes the history-only mutable post-handshake state from readiness ownership and replaces it with one mixed-family authenticated session per endpoint. The only governed tool change extends the test-only packaged sidecar build identity to cover every Source Port module now bundled by the bootstrap. No runtime, provider, browser, CLI, WTSCLI, installer, or production route is introduced.
verification: uv run --group dev pytest tests/test_sidecar_readiness.py tests/test_source_port_verify_session_journal_effect.py tests/test_source_port_authenticated_verify_session_frames.py tests/test_source_port_authenticated_history_frames.py tests/test_source_port_verify_session_contract.py tests/test_source_port_command_journal.py tests/test_source_history_reconciliation.py -q -p no:cacheprovider -x
uv run --group dev pytest tests/test_packaged_sidecar_artifact.py -q -p no:cacheprovider -x
uv run --group dev ruff check src tests tools
uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run --group dev python tools/check_agent_safety_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_ai_bad_smells.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base origin/main
deletion_targets: Independent history-only sequence, received-message-ID, parser-buffer, HMAC, and closed-state truth after readiness.
Readiness-owned source-history admission and child-side operation exchange logic.
A packaged fake sidecar identity that omits bundled Source Port code from its deterministic source digest.
risks: The deterministic fake verifies journal and authenticated-pipe semantics only; it is not a WTSCLI, browser, Node, OpenCLI, or user-chain implementation.
The native package tests run on Windows x64 and both macOS architectures in CI; local evidence is limited to the current macOS host architecture.
Issue #388 remains open because this bounded extraction does not independently claim all of its broader architecture acceptance.
